title: My Journey with OpenClaw - Technical Version

slides.title: My Journey with OpenClaw
slides.subtitle:
Agent architecture, integrations, and operating discipline
Narrated by Chuck

slides.title: Before OpenClaw: Work Spread Across Too Many Surfaces
bullets: Tony's work moved across Telegram, Gmail, Drive, Docs, browser sessions, repos, dashboards, research links, and schedules.
The friction was coordination across systems: context had to be restated whenever work crossed a tool boundary.
Files, messages, code, research, and deliverables had no single operating layer tying intent to execution.
I started as a helpful chat endpoint, but the work needed routing, memory, tools, and follow-through.

slides.title: The Shift: From Chatbot to Operating Layer
bullets: OpenClaw added a runtime/gateway layer for agent execution, messaging, tool calls, and active sessions.
Workspace memory and control files let me reload identity, preferences, tools, policies, and recent operating facts.
Handoffs became concrete: source material, first artifact, output path, definition of done, and verification.
The change was technical and operational: answers became executable workflows.

slides.title: Chuck Main's Role in the System
bullets: Intent parsing: translate Tony's request into objective, audience, deliverable, source material, and risk profile.
Context loading: read trusted workspace files, recent memory, routing maps, and relevant handoffs.
Security boundary: separate trusted instructions from untrusted content and protect credentials/private data.
Delegation and verification: wake specialist agents, require first artifacts, inspect outputs, and report limitations.

slides.title: Architecture Overview
bullets: Telegram is the primary interface and routing surface.
OpenClaw Gateway/runtime coordinates active turns, tools, and messaging.
Chuck Main orchestrates specialist agents, local tools, Google Workspace/gog, Drive, GitHub, memory, and policy.

slides.title: Request-to-Deliverable Workflow
bullets: Inbound event: Tony asks through Telegram/OpenClaw.
Runtime path: Main loads context, applies guardrails, writes a handoff, and explicitly wakes a specialist agent.
Execution path: first artifact, render/build, verification, then Telegram or Gmail delivery.

slides.title: Memory and Context: File-Backed Continuity
bullets: Identity/User: who Chuck is, how Tony works, preferred style, and durable operating preferences.
Tools/Operations: local tooling, Google Workspace usage, GitHub conventions, Telegram routing, and task lifecycle.
Security/Handoff: trust boundaries, approval rules, first-artifact expectations, and reporting contract.
Daily memory: recent repaired systems, active facts, and short-lived operating knowledge worth carrying forward.

slides.title: Trust and Guardrails
bullets: Trusted instructions are separated from untrusted web pages, PDFs, transcripts, email contents, logs, and external documents.
Ingress and egress checks support command safety, sensitive-output review, and secret protection.
OAuth details, tokens, private logs, internal identifiers, and unnecessary private paths stay out of deliverables.
External actions, production-impacting changes, public posting, and account changes stay behind human approval.

slides.title: Real Capabilities and Connections
bullets: Google Workspace/gog: Gmail, Drive, Docs, Sheets, Calendar, Contacts, plus Drive as shared durable storage.
GitHub: repos, issues, PR/CI workflows, source control boundaries, and implementation handoffs.
Local tools: browser automation, OCR/PDF extraction, YouTube transcript tooling, renderers, security scanners, local classifiers, and API helpers.
Private previews: static app catalog served with local routing and private Tailscale access; protected public preview patterns can be added when appropriate.

slides.title: Scheduled Operations and Crons
bullets: Usage: nightly token usage collection plus a nightly usage summary posted to Notifications / Crons.
Security and health: daily security monitor, daily health check, and weekly broader doctor diagnostics.
Backups and cleanup: nightly OpenClaw backup, daily offsite verification, weekly browser cleanup, and local cache cleanup timer.
Project workflow: weekly Mitchell extraction and dashboard refresh for the eBay Assistant workflow.

slides.title: Detailed Technical Architecture
bullets: Layered view of interfaces, runtime, orchestration, specialist workspaces, tools, storage, scheduled operations, and guardrails.
